codeReview.summary: Restore flow wiring is substantial, but two blocking correctness gaps remain: the claimed multi-device VAL-CROSS-007 coverage is not executable as written, and relay querying is not actually parallel so a slow first relay can mask a valid backup on later relays.

issues.description: The spec hard-codes `RELAY_URL` as `ws://127.0.0.1:8194`, but restore calls go through `restoreProfileFromRelay` which validates every relay via `validateRelayUrl` and rejects non-`wss://` URLs before any REQ is emitted (`src/app/AppStateProvider.tsx:2836`, `src/lib/relay/relayUrl.ts:64,73`). As a result, the first restore call in this spec cannot succeed, so VAL-CROSS-007/VAL-BACKUP-010..030 coverage claimed by this test is not actually achieved.

Relay fan-out is implemented with a sequential loop (`for ... await conn.connect()`), not parallel connect/subscribe. Because the timeout is global (5s at line 2898) and `BrowserRelayConnection.connect()` has no built-in timeout (`src/lib/relay/browserRelayClient.ts:217-258`), a slow/hung earlier relay can consume the window and prevent later relays from ever receiving REQ subscriptions. This violates VAL-BACKUP-009's expected per-relay query behavior and can yield false "No backup found" failures despite an available backup on another relay.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-screen-worker procedure requires a RED test run capture and `npx playwright test src/e2e/demo-gallery.spec.ts --project=desktop --workers 1` in verification, but this feature handoff marks `followedProcedure: true` while reporting only typecheck + vitest.

Mission docs recommend local relay fallback for multi-device validation, but restore flow enforces `wss://` inputs while `services.local_relay` exposes a plain ws endpoint on 8194. This protocol mismatch is not documented and directly contributed to the broken restore e2e.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Fallback guidance: `AGENTS.md:86,89`; local relay definition: `.factory/services.yaml:36-40`; restore validation: `src/lib/relay/relayUrl.ts:64,73`.

summary: Reviewed feature commit and transcript artifacts; status is FAIL due to two blocking issues: (1) restore multi-device e2e uses `ws://` and cannot pass against enforced `wss://` restore validation, and (2) restore relay querying is sequential (not parallel), so a slow first relay can cause false 'No backup found' outcomes.
